summary: Five unit tests plus one parameterized integration test (11 PG versions, with t.Skipf for unavailable ones). Unit tests verify SQL template structure (column order, required clauses, conditional ShowNoIdle, embedded QueryAgeThresh); behavioral correctness is verified by the integration test which actually executes the formatted query against PostgreSQL. The substring-presence assertions in unit tests are appropriate here because (a) the task's contract IS the SQL template structure — downstream tasks index columns positionally, and (b) live PG execution provides the behavioral signal.

litmusTest.rationale: If the SQL constant were broken (wrong column order, missing clause, malformed template), unit tests would fail on substring/ordering assertions AND the integration test would fail with a Postgres error. Removing any single test removes a specific signal: ShowNoIdle toggling, default QueryAgeThresh value, custom QueryAgeThresh value, column order, integration smoke against each PG version.

checks.tddAnchorQuality: pass — substring assertions are scoped to SQL structural contract that downstream tasks depend on; behavioral verification delegated to integration test (real PG execution).

litmusPerTest.signal: column order + presence of required clauses; if SELECT list reordered, the ordered-substring loop fails
ShowNoIdle=false branch suppresses idle filter (guards against accidental {{ end }} omission)
custom threshold value is embedded verbatim
NewOptions default threshold is embedded even without explicit set (guards against accidental {{ if }} guard added later)

formatted query is a syntactically valid SQL statement on every supported PG version; catches regressions in regexp_replace escape syntax, alias names, and pg_stat_activity column existence per version

checks.redundantTesting: no — each test catches a specific failure mode no other catches
checks.pyramidBalance: appropriate for SQL constant: small unit surface, integration covers cross-version behavior
